entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The target crop for biology research and genetic improvement

entities.name: TRAIT
entities.description: Characteristics of interest in crops that are targeted for discovery and improvement

entities.name: SEQUENCING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used to generate sequence data, characterized by reducing costs and increasing speed

entities.name: GENOTYPING PLATFORMS
entities.description: Platforms used for genotyping that may become obsolete due to low-cost sequencing

entities.name: SINGLE GENOME SEQUENCE
entities.description: The approach of sequencing a single representative genome of a crop

entities.name: PANGENOMES
entities.description: The assembly of multiple genomes to represent the full genetic diversity of a species

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of precise measurement of traits in segregating populations and germplasm collections

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The measure of genetic variation present within germplasm collections

entities.name: GENE
entities.description: The genetic unit or nucleotide associated with specific traits

entities.name: MULTI-PARENT POPULATIONS
entities.description: Populations derived from multiple parents used for trait discovery and haplotype identification

entities.name: BI-PARENTAL POPULATIONS
entities.description: Populations derived from two parents, used as a precursor to multi-parent populations

entities.name: HAPLOTYPES
entities.description: Specific combinations of alleles identified as superior for trait improvement

entities.name: FUNCTIONAL VARIATION
entities.description: Variation in genes that controls traits of interest

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A technology used to manipulate genes for crop improvement based on markers

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A technology used to manipulate genes for crop improvement using genome-wide markers

entities.name: GENOME EDITING
entities.description: A technology used to routinely manipulate genes for crop improvement
